summary: Iteration 63: Verified Bayesian-AI Fusion architecture change. All 28 pytest tests passed (17 Bayesian Engine + 11 Fusion). The critical fix fuses Bayesian posterior INTO the Grok/GPT-5.2 projection BEFORE calibration runs, creating one unified engine. Pipeline order verified: AI → Bayesian Compute → Fusion → Calibration Guards → Elite Calibration → Final Guard. Old display-only Bayesian block removed. consensusNote updated to 'Unified engine: ... + Bayesian math + elite calibration'. fusionApplied metadata includes all transparency fields. Health endpoint returns ok. Frontend loads without console errors.
seed_data_creation: None - used synthetic test data in unit tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 63 verified the Bayesian-AI Fusion architecture change. Key changes: (1) Bayesian Engine now computes BEFORE fusion (predict.py line 1973, basketball_predict.py line 930), (2) Fusion merges AI projection with Bayesian posterior using adaptive weights (agree=40/60, disagree+strong=50/50, disagree+moderate=40/60, disagree+weak=30/70), (3) fusionApplied metadata contains full transparency (aiProjection, bayesianPosterior, weights, agreement), (4) consensusNote says 'Unified engine' instead of just 'Grok 4.20 analysis', (5) Old post-calibration Bayesian display block removed. The 3 real contradiction test cases (Whiteman, Klarer, Randell) all pass - fusion correctly resolves AI/Bayesian disagreements.
